_meta.purpose: Floating microdrama concept registry for Dear.AI. /microdramaideas pulls from this. Edit freely — add new ideas, mark status, archive ones we've dropped.

ideas.tagline_en: 1880s Wild West where magic is the establishment — one magicless gunslinger fights back with a Colt.
ideas.tagline_id: Old West tahun 1880-an di mana sihir adalah kekuasaan — seorang penembak tanpa sihir melawan balik dengan revolver.
ideas.genre: fantasy western · war-god trope · prestige drama
ideas.lead: Cole Walker — Caucasian ex-Union cavalry vet, mid-30s, magicless, hunting the mage who burned his family's homestead
ideas.dopamine_engine: Public humiliation → hidden capability → reveal cycle every ep. Cole's revolver bypasses magical defenses (they were never designed against bullets). Audience figures it out in Ep 1, every subsequent humiliation = pre-loaded payoff.
key_cast: Cole Walker (protagonist)
Nel Cobb (widow schoolteacher, love interest)
Tomás Cobb (Nel's magicless 9yo son)
Mireya Vasquez (Mexican-American hex doctor, ally)
Marshal Jericho Boone (Black retired US Marshal, mentor)
Lula Marshall (Black saloon owner, info broker)
Speaks-To-Stone (Apache elder)
Aldous Whitmore (British-American villain magus)
Wren Whitmore (his daughter, femme fatale)
Sheriff Henry Doyle (corrupt magicless sheriff)
Mason Bell (charlatan showman mage)
Jack 'Drifter' Mercer (older magicless gunslinger, Cole's possible future)
ideas.why_team: War-god trope universal. Cheap to shoot in Bali/Yogya with frontier dressing. SE Asian or Caucasian lead both work. Censor-friendly metaphor for corrupt establishment.
ideas.scale: 60-90s vertical microdrama OR limited series (Succession-meets-Unforgiven if budgeted up)
ideas.notes: Ensemble image generated 2026-05-13 via nano_banana_2. Cole Walker described as Caucasian per team request. 12-character cast ready.

ideas.tagline_en: A Jakarta housemaid witnesses her American expat boss murder his business partner — and discovers everyone she could turn to is already on his payroll.
ideas.tagline_id: Seorang pembantu di Jakarta menyaksikan majikan ekspatriat Amerika-nya membunuh mitra bisnisnya — dan menyadari semua orang yang bisa dia hubungi sudah dibayar oleh majikannya.
ideas.genre: class thriller · Parasite × I Care a Lot × Get Out
ideas.lead: Sari Wulan — 26, Javanese domestic worker, 4 years employed by the Hendersons
ideas.dopamine_engine: Audience roots for Sari from frame 1. Cleanest underdog setup possible. Forced isolation (no police, no embassy, no home). Coalition-building one ally per ep. Every ep ends mid-escape, mid-betrayal, or mid-reveal.
key_cast: Sari Wulan (protagonist, the maid)
Tom Henderson (American villain, charming on camera)
Karen Henderson (his wife, knows more than she admits)
Eko Pratama (investigative journalist at Tempo, love interest)
Detective Made Wijaya (Balinese cop, corrupt-but-honourable)
Hannah Reeves (American war photographer, Tom's ex)
Wati Wulan (Sari's nursing-student sister, the reason for everything)
Pak Sutadi (Indonesian businessman, the real villain, funded Wati's scholarship)
ideas.why_team: Universally legible class thriller. Lean cast, contained Jakarta locations. Festival-friendly AND microdrama-friendly. Sari role is a career-launcher.
ideas.scale: 10-ep microdrama OR 6-ep prestige limited series (same script, different pacing)
ideas.notes: Likely first to greenlight per team preference. Cheapest to shoot. Strongest US-streamer pitch.

ideas.tagline_en: A billionaire-heiress from Jakarta is exiled to New York with $200 and 90 days to survive without her name — and falls for a working-class line cook who has no idea who she is.
ideas.tagline_id: Pewaris miliarder dari Jakarta diasingkan ke New York dengan $200 dan 90 hari untuk bertahan hidup tanpa namanya — dan jatuh cinta pada seorang juru masak kelas pekerja yang tidak tahu siapa dia.
ideas.lead: Mei-Lin Tjandra — 23, Indonesian-Chinese heiress to a palm-oil dynasty
ideas.setting: Bushwick NYC + Jakarta mansion · contemporary, dual-location
ideas.dopamine_engine: Cinderella in reverse: the princess hides her identity, audience knows, love interest doesn't. Reveal-trigger every ep (magazine, doorman, gala). Marcus arc gives stakes (mother, brother, dream). The Reveal Ep gut-punch at Ep 7. Day-90 countdown visible every ep.
key_cast: Mei-Lin Tjandra (heiress, the lie)
Marcus Walker (Black American line cook, the truth)
Bart 'Pak' Tjandra (Mei-Lin's father, complicated tycoon villain)
Carmen Walker (Marcus's retired-nurse mother, the moral compass)
Devon (Marcus's younger brother, on probation, the rage)
Sienna + Yuki + Trish (Bushwick roommates, comic relief turned ride-or-dies)
Stefan Halim (Mei-Lin's Jakarta ex-fiancé, real antagonist)
Chef Antoine (Marcus's culinary mentor)
ideas.why_team: Two markets in one (Jakarta dynasty drama + American working-class romance). Cheap-medium budget. Iconic star-launcher for both leads. Endless season hooks.
ideas.notes: Second priority after Pembantu. Romance has been done, but reverse-Cinderella + financial-class-clash combo feels fresh in this market.

archived.reason_archived: Atomized prematurely without user approval. Sheet trashed 2026-05-12. Concept seed-of-DRY-COUNTRY: 'skilled soldier in world of witches and wizards humiliated until he gets a gun.'
Workshopped briefly. Modern-day spec ops in supernatural world. Less character-driven than DRY COUNTRY direction.
Earlier name for the concept that became DRY COUNTRY. Team felt original term was too campy.
Strong cross-cultural premise (American vulcanologist + Indo ritual bride). Still viable — could reactivate. Filed for later.
